title: nigrina sessiliflora
intro: Nigrina sessiliflora, also known as the Black-eyed Susan or Leopard's Bane, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Nigrina sessiliflora include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic to treat urinary problems. Nigrina sessiliflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of the world.
